MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Compel Third Party Witness John Motgomery To Answer Questions And To Produce Documents Pursuant To Deposition Subpoena
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tiffany R. Norman, Esq., TRN Law Associates, Telephone 415-8234566, appearing for Plaintiff;
Ray Z. Bacerdo, Bishop Barry, Telephone 510-596-2888, appearing for Defendant Mark Strehlow;
Gregory Kim, Esq., Yang Professionsal Law Corporation, telephone 626-921-4300, appearing for Defendant Mary Nichols;

RULING - ARGUED: The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Mary Nichols' motion to compel third party witness John Montgomery to answer questions and to produce documents pursuant to deposition subpoena is granted. Mr. Montgomery is required to attend a further session of his deposition not exceeding three hours and may not refuse to answer any questions on the grounds of attorney-client privilege or attorney work product regarding his communications with and work for defendant Mark Strehelow pertaining to the Waller Street property including all facts and opinions stated in his declaration filed in this case. Neither Mr. Montgomery nor Mr. Strehelow has sufficiently shown that any communications that Mr. Montgomery had with or work he performed on behalf of Mr. Strehelow were in Mr. Montgomery's capacity as an attorney. Mr. Montgomery must produce all responsive documents called for in the subpoena at least three business days before the next session of his deposition and may not withhold any documents on the grounds of either attorney-client privilege or attorney work product. Request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,600.00 payable to Plaintiff within 90 days or on the date of judgment whichever comes earlier. Prevailing party to prepare and submit form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MARK STREHLOW'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Appearances: Joseph S. Tobener and Rahman Popal of Tobener Law (415.504.2165) for defendant and moving party; Tiffany R. Norman of TRN Law Associates (415.823.4566) for plaintiff and responding party.

This matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. As an initial matter, the Court has not ruled on the merits of the original demurrer. In the March 29, 2016 Order (approved as to form by all parties) the Court indicated that the hearing on the Demurrer will be held at a later date. The hearing did not take place on that date due to the pending appeal. Defendant filed his demurrer after remittitur. Opposition and Reply on this round of briefing is different from the papers submitted for the original demurrer. Additionally, based on Tobener declaration there has been a significant change in circumstances, specifically that Defendant amended his Estoppel Certificate and does not claim disability anymore. Based on the new briefing the Court rules as follows:
Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is overruled. According to the Rent Board rules (See Ex. A to Norman Declaration) the Rent Board will not provide a declaration regarding disability. Sending this case to the Rent Board would be a futile act. However, the Plaintiff should not re-allege this cause of action in the amended complaint if there is no ripe controversy; it appears based on Tobener declaration that Defendant does not claim disability any longer.
Demurrer to causes of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage and negligent interference with prospective economic advantage is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to identify "in some manner" a "specific" buyer. Ramona Manor v. Care Enterprises (1986) 177 Cal.3d 1120. Demurrer to these causes of action is otherwise overruled.
Demurrer to the cause of action for elder abuse is overruled. This cause of action is sufficiently alleged. Bounds v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 229 Cal.App.4th 468; Mahan v. Charles W. Chan Insurance Agency (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 841. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Brittany Erickson, CSR 13868 (Litivate Reporting & Trial Services, Tel: 877.771.3312) (501/AJR)

Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Josph Tobener, (415)504-2165 for Defendant/Moving party Mark Strehlow.
Tiffany Norman, (415)823-4566 for Plaintiff/Opposing party Mary Nichols.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MARK STREHLOW Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Complaint For Declaratory Relief, Intentional Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage, And Negligent Interference With Prospective Economic Advantage is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden under CCP 425.16's first prong. Tenant's claim for disability is not protected activity within the meaning of CCP 425.16(e)(1) or (4). Tenant's claim is not a conduct in "connection with a public issue or an issue of public interest." Tenant's claim also was not made in "relat[ion] to itigation that is contemplated in good faith and under serious consideration." Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).
